codeReview.summary: The fix successfully addresses two prior blockers (non-string operator crash guard and dedupe ordering after ID normalization), but the null-coercion blocker from post-processor-module is not fully resolved: multiple schema-required top-level string fields still remain null after post-processing.

issues.description: _coerce_null_strings still only coerces selected fields (starting with title at line 137 and data subfields), but does not coerce required string fields `source.standard`, `source.section`, `metadata.extracted_by`, or `metadata.qc_status` (schema requires string at schema/element.schema.json lines 22, 24, 49, 53). Reproduced with a local check: passing an element with those fields set to null through `post_process` leaves them as null (`{'standard': None, 'section': None}` and `{'extracted_by': None, 'qc_status': None}`), so the original blocking null-coercion gap remains.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Procedure adherence reporting is still inconsistent for python-worker: the skill requires test-first TDD, but this run implemented fixes before adding tests while handoff marked followedProcedure=true with no deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/python-worker/SKILL.md line 29 requires "Write tests first (TDD)"; transcript skeleton for workerSessionId 4c1706a0-7eed-4d1a-a65b-b707ad58a74a states "Now let me implement all three fixes" before "Now add test cases", while handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true and deviations=[].

summary: Re-run review for fix-scrutiny-post-processor-bugs: non-string operator handling and dedupe-after-post-process are fixed, but null coercion remains incomplete for required top-level schema string fields, so this fix does not fully clear the prior blocking failure set.
